research_question: Collaborative project with Virtanen Lab based on a single-center, prospective cohort trial (ISRCTN91953024) of 258 women under 31 years old with CIN2 managed with active surveillance and followed for up to 24 months. The project aims to identify spatial TME and transcriptomic differences between pro
project_summary: Collaborative project with Virtanen Lab based on a single-center, prospective cohort trial (ISRCTN91953024) of 258 women under 31 years old with CIN2 managed with active surveillance and followed for up to 24 months. The project aims to identify spatial TME and transcriptomic differences between progressive, regressive, or persistent lesions. 67 biopsies from 60 patients were selected (15 quick regressors in 1 year, 15 slow regressors in 2 years, 30 non-regressors, plus 3 matched invasive carcinoma progressions). Integrates multi-pass t-CycIF proteomics (Ella/Naipunya/Saundarya) and GeoMx spatial transcriptomics. Implements cell segmentation via Oskari Lehtonen's pipeline, Tribus cell calling, spatial Recurrent Cellular Neighborhoods (RCN) mapping (DBSCAN/spatial count at 100um), spatial LDA, and correlates cell type proportions with clinical outcomes, HPV high-risk/low-risk types (including HPV16 status), and E4 viral replication relocation status. **Description:** Collaborative project with Virtanen Lab based on a single-center, prospective cohort trial (ISRCTN91953024) of 258 women under 31 years old with CIN2 managed with active surveillance and followed for up to 24 months. The project aims to identify spatial TME and transcriptomic differences between progressive, regressive, or persistent lesions. 67 biopsies from 60 patients were selected (15 quick regressors in 1 year, 15 slow regressors in 2 years, 30 non-regressors, plus 3 matched invasive carcinoma progressions). Integrates multi-pass t-CycIF proteomics (Ella/Naipunya/Saundarya) and GeoMx spatial transcriptomics. Implements cell segmentation via Oskari Lehtonen's pipeline, Tribus cell calling, spatial Recurrent Cellular Neighborhoods (RCN) mapping (DBSCAN/spatial count at 100um), spatial LDA, and correlates cell type proportions with clinical outcomes, HPV high-risk/low-risk types (including HPV16 status), and E4 viral replication relocation status.

research_question: Collaborative project with the Sorger Lab (Harvard Systems Pharmacology) profiling oxidative stress and hypoxia in triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) to classify metabolically distinct environments and their associated immune cell populations. The study utilizes 9 multiplexed t-CycIF TMA images (o
project_summary: Collaborative project with the Sorger Lab (Harvard Systems Pharmacology) profiling oxidative stress and hypoxia in triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) to classify metabolically distinct environments and their associated immune cell populations. The study utilizes 9 multiplexed t-CycIF TMA images (originally 13) containing 345 cores (originally 368 cores, representing 3.68M cells total) from a cohort of 186 TNBC patients (stage 1–4 with annotated clinical survival). The 34-antibody t-CycIF panel includes specific oxidative stress/antioxidant and functional markers (GCLC, NQO1, TXNRD1, GLUT1, Ki67, PCNA, pS6, E-cadherin, pRB, pSTAT3, Vimentin, H2ax, H3K27me3). Analyzes how antioxidant marker levels (GCLC, NQO1, TXNRD1) define high/low stress niches, evaluates EMT-like metabolic states in tumor niches (GCLC+ Vimentin+ cancer cells vs other tumor cells), maps spatial Recurrent Cellular Neighborhoods (RCNs), and integrates findings with public single-cell RNA-seq and Visium spatial transcriptomics datasets. **Description:** Collaborative project with the Sorger Lab (Harvard Systems Pharmacology) profiling oxidative stress and hypoxia in triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) to classify metabolically distinct environments and their associated immune cell populations. The study utilizes 9 multiplexed t-CycIF TMA images (originally 13) containing 345 cores (originally 368 cores, representing 3.68M cells total) from a cohort of 186 TNBC patients (stage 1–4 with annotated clinical survival). The 34-antibody t-CycIF panel includes specific oxidative stress/antioxidant and functional markers (GCLC, NQO1, TXNRD1, GLUT1, Ki67, PCNA, pS6, E-cadherin, pRB, pSTAT3, Vimentin, H2ax, H3K27me3). Analyzes how antioxidant marker levels (GCLC, NQO1, TXNRD1) define high/low stress niches, evaluates EMT-like metabolic states in tumor niches (GCLC+ Vimentin+ cancer cells vs other tumor cells), maps spatial Recurrent Cellular Neighborhoods (RCNs), and integrates findings with public single-cell RNA-seq and Visium spatial transcriptomics datasets.

research_question: : Analysis of a large cohort of 265 HGSC patients using 10 TMAs (four cores per tumor) to profile HLA-class II expression on cancer cells and its association with survival and clinical outcomes. This study was provisionally accepted/published in *Cancer Discovery* 2026 (doi:10.1158/2159-8290.CD-25-1
project_summary: : Analysis of a large cohort of 265 HGSC patients using 10 TMAs (four cores per tumor) to profile HLA-class II expression on cancer cells and its association with survival and clinical outcomes. This study was provisionally accepted/published in *Cancer Discovery* 2026 (doi:10.1158/2159-8290.CD-25-1492). Integrates 30-antibody t-CycIF proteomics, Whole Slide Image (WSI) validations from the Oncosys-OVA (HRPritti) cohort, and GeoMx Spatial Transcriptomics. Custom preprocessing was developed using OpenCV denoising, Ilastik (DAPI+LaminB) pixel classification, CellProfiler segmentation, and Tribus cell-type calling. MultiNicheNetR ligand-receptor profiling was performed to map tumor-immune cell interactions (CD4+, CD8+, regulatory T cells, DCs vs tumor cells) in MHCII-expressing niches, validated via GeoMx. Includes immunopeptidomic profiling (with Cerullo's laboratory) of MHCII peptides from 12 HGSC study samples (Anastasia). **Description**: Analysis of a large cohort of 265 HGSC patients using 10 TMAs (four cores per tumor) to profile HLA-class II expression on cancer cells and its association with survival and clinical outcomes. This study was provisionally accepted/published in *Cancer Discovery* 2026 (doi:10.1158/2159-8290.CD-25-1492). Integrates 30-antibody t-CycIF proteomics, Whole Slide Image (WSI) validations from the Oncosys-OVA (HRPritti) cohort, and GeoMx Spatial Transcriptomics. Custom preprocessing was developed using OpenCV denoising, Ilastik (DAPI+LaminB) pixel classification, CellProfiler segmentation, and Tribus cell-type calling. MultiNicheNetR ligand-receptor profiling was performed to map tumor-immune cell interactions (CD4+, CD8+, regulatory T cells, DCs vs tumor cells) in MHCII-expressing niches, validated via GeoMx. Includes immunopeptidomic profiling (with Cerullo's laboratory) of MHCII peptides from 12 HGSC study samples (Anastasia).

research_question: **Description: \[Published\]** Custom computational package featuring a knowledge-based, multi-stage single-cell classification algorithm that uses logical gating trees of marker expressions to annotate cell types in multiplexed imaging datasets (e.g. t-CycIF) without manual labeling or hard thresho
project_summary: **Description: \[Published\]** Custom computational package featuring a knowledge-based, multi-stage single-cell classification algorithm that uses logical gating trees of marker expressions to annotate cell types in multiplexed imaging datasets (e.g. t-CycIF) without manual labeling or hard thresholds. Published in *Bioinformatics* (doi:10.1093/bioinformatics/btaf082). Benchmarked against three state-of-the-art classifiers on four public datasets with ground truth, demonstrating superior accuracy and a runtime reduction of an order of magnitude. Performance validated on two large whole-slide imaging datasets from the lab. Code is open-sourced at [farkkilab/tribus](https://github.com/farkkilab/tribus), with benchmarking data and notebooks hosted on [Synapse (syn63884734)](https://www.synapse.org/Synapse:syn63884734).

research_question: Deep learning computational pipeline utilizing a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) and Vector Quantized VAE (VQ-VAE) model (Tissue Microenvironment Encoder - TMEncoder) to extract explainable latent embeddings directly from multi-channel multiplexed images (multiplex CycIF) without prior cell segmentati
project_summary: Deep learning computational pipeline utilizing a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) and Vector Quantized VAE (VQ-VAE) model (Tissue Microenvironment Encoder - TMEncoder) to extract explainable latent embeddings directly from multi-channel multiplexed images (multiplex CycIF) without prior cell segmentation. Reduces 30+ channel dimensions into under 10 dimensions representing tissue archetypes. Applied to unsupervised image profiling (spatial motifs and recurrent cellular neighborhood mapping) and automatic ROI selection for spatial transcriptomics (GeoMx/Xenium) using latent space embeddings, downstream classifiers, and Grad-CAM visualization. Merged as a core component of Project 12: SPACE. **Description:** Deep learning computational pipeline utilizing a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) and Vector Quantized VAE (VQ-VAE) model (Tissue Microenvironment Encoder - TMEncoder) to extract explainable latent embeddings directly from multi-channel multiplexed images (multiplex CycIF) without prior cell segmentation. Reduces 30+ channel dimensions into under 10 dimensions representing tissue archetypes. Applied to unsupervised image profiling (spatial motifs and recurrent cellular neighborhood mapping) and automatic ROI selection for spatial transcriptomics (GeoMx/Xenium) using latent space embeddings, downstream classifiers, and Grad-CAM visualization. Merged as a core component of Project 12: SPACE.

research_question: An integrated, reproducible computational pipeline to model overall survival (OS) and progression-free survival (PFS) in ovarian cancer by combining multi-modal patient-level data. Integrates clinical labels (stage, drug combinations), genomic profiles (WES, HRD/HRP ratios, CNA status for CCNE1, MYC
project_summary: An integrated, reproducible computational pipeline to model overall survival (OS) and progression-free survival (PFS) in ovarian cancer by combining multi-modal patient-level data. Integrates clinical labels (stage, drug combinations), genomic profiles (WES, HRD/HRP ratios, CNA status for CCNE1, MYC, MECOM from iMTB report), and multiplexed spatial proteomics features (derived from t-CycIF via SPACEstat). Employs survival models (Kaplan-Meier, Cox proportional hazards, Lasso regression), feature engineering (Variance Inflation Factor [VIF] redundancy checks, correlation heatmaps), and MOFA2 (Multi-Omics Factor Analysis) for unsupervised multi-modal integration. Spatial inputs include RCN proportions (RCN3, RCN9, RCN11), global/local immune-TSI distances, immune infiltration index (tumor vs stroma densities), nearest neighbor distance (NND), cluster density, and Gini density. Results are integrated directly with Project 12: SPACE. **Description:** An integrated, reproducible computational pipeline to model overall survival (OS) and progression-free survival (PFS) in ovarian cancer by combining multi-modal patient-level data. Integrates clinical labels (stage, drug combinations), genomic profiles (WES, HRD/HRP ratios, CNA status for CCNE1, MYC, MECOM from iMTB report), and multiplexed spatial proteomics features (derived from t-CycIF via SPACEstat). Employs survival models (Kaplan-Meier, Cox proportional hazards, Lasso regression), feature engineering (Variance Inflation Factor [VIF] redundancy checks, correlation heatmaps), and MOFA2 (Multi-Omics Factor Analysis) for unsupervised multi-modal integration. Spatial inputs include RCN proportions (RCN3, RCN9, RCN11), global/local immune-TSI distances, immune infiltration index (tumor vs stroma densities), nearest neighbor distance (NND), cluster density, and Gini density. Results are integrated directly with Project 12: SPACE.

research_question: An automated Python/R spatial statistics software framework designed for quantitative profiling of the tumor microenvironment (TME) in large whole-slide multiplexed images (such as t-CycIF). Features include immune residency identification (tumor vs stroma local context classification parallelized d
project_summary: An automated Python/R spatial statistics software framework designed for quantitative profiling of the tumor microenvironment (TME) in large whole-slide multiplexed images (such as t-CycIF). Features include immune residency identification (tumor vs stroma local context classification parallelized down to 2 minutes), tumor-stroma interface (TSI) contour mapping and continuous distance metrics, nearest neighbor distance (NND), cluster density, and Gini diversity. Incorporates advanced distance-based network modeling (Delaunay triangulation with distance cutoffs) to map single-cell and multi-cell type immune hubs (co-aggregates of up to 5 immune cell types), analyzing hub interaction and repulsion networks using PySAL, NetworkX, Rtree, and KDTree. Serves as the primary spatial statistics engine for Projects 12 and 9, with benchmarking validated on public CODEX and MIBI-TOF datasets. Available at [Kkkzq/SPACEstat](https://github.com/Kkkzq/SPACEstat/tree/main). **Description:** An automated Python/R spatial statistics software framework designed for quantitative profiling of the tumor microenvironment (TME) in large whole-slide multiplexed images (such as t-CycIF). Features include immune residency identification (tumor vs stroma local context classification parallelized down to 2 minutes), tumor-stroma interface (TSI) contour mapping and continuous distance metrics, nearest neighbor distance (NND), cluster density, and Gini diversity. Incorporates advanced distance-based network modeling (Delaunay triangulation with distance cutoffs) to map single-cell and multi-cell type immune hubs (co-aggregates of up to 5 immune cell types), analyzing hub interaction and repulsion networks using PySAL, NetworkX, Rtree, and KDTree. Serves as the primary spatial statistics engine for Projects 12 and 9, with benchmarking validated on public CODEX and MIBI-TOF datasets. Available at [Kkkzq/SPACEstat](https://github.com/Kkkzq/SPACEstat/tree/main).

research_question: Spatiotemporal and molecular mapping of antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) targets (such as VTCN1 and other clinical candidates) in the HGSC microenvironment. The project integrates multi-pass t-CycIF spatial proteomics (Andreas/Saundarya), 58 GeoMx spatial transcriptomics samples (preprocessed/deconvolu
project_summary: Spatiotemporal and molecular mapping of antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) targets (such as VTCN1 and other clinical candidates) in the HGSC microenvironment. The project integrates multi-pass t-CycIF spatial proteomics (Andreas/Saundarya), 58 GeoMx spatial transcriptomics samples (preprocessed/deconvoluted by Iga Niemiec), bulk RNA-seq deconvolution (PRISM/CyberPRISM compartment profiling by Anni), and Xenium spatial transcriptomics. Computational workflows include batch correction (ComBat), target specificity differential expression (limma tumor vs stroma), Spearman co-expression analysis, extreme group DE profiling (comparing high vs low ADC target expressors), and defining spatial metrics to measure inter-patient and intra-tumoral heterogeneity of ADC targets. **Description:** Spatiotemporal and molecular mapping of antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) targets (such as VTCN1 and other clinical candidates) in the HGSC microenvironment. The project integrates multi-pass t-CycIF spatial proteomics (Andreas/Saundarya), 58 GeoMx spatial transcriptomics samples (preprocessed/deconvoluted by Iga Niemiec), bulk RNA-seq deconvolution (PRISM/CyberPRISM compartment profiling by Anni), and Xenium spatial transcriptomics. Computational workflows include batch correction (ComBat), target specificity differential expression (limma tumor vs stroma), Spearman co-expression analysis, extreme group DE profiling (comparing high vs low ADC target expressors), and defining spatial metrics to measure inter-patient and intra-tumoral heterogeneity of ADC targets.
